name: ◁ Iraqi Timman BagillaFAVA BEAN AND DILL RICE

ingredients.item: 3 cups (600 g) long-grain white basmati rice, rinsed until the water runs clear and drained completely
ingredients.original: 3 cups (600 g) long-grain white basmati rice, rinsed until the water runs clear and drained completely

ingredients.item: 28 ounces (800 g) frozen cooked and peeled fava beans (or substitute cooked or canned lima beans)
ingredients.original: 28 ounces (800 g) frozen cooked and peeled fava beans (or substitute cooked or canned lima beans)

ingredients.item: 3 cups (150 g) packed finely chopped fresh dill, thick stems removed
ingredients.original: 3 cups (150 g) packed finely chopped fresh dill, thick stems removed

ingredients.item: 4 cups (960 ml) chicken or vegetable broth, divided
ingredients.original: 4 cups (960 ml) chicken or vegetable broth, divided

ingredients.item: 1 Place the rinsed rice in a bowl, cover with water, and let soak for 10 minutes.
ingredients.original: 1 Place the rinsed rice in a bowl, cover with water, and let soak for 10 minutes.

ingredients.item: 2 In a medium pot, heat 5 tablespoons of the oil over medium heat. Add the onion and cook, stirring often, until softened and translucent, 5 to 7 minutes.
ingredients.original: 2 In a medium pot, heat 5 tablespoons of the oil over medium heat. Add the onion and cook, stirring often, until softened and translucent, 5 to 7 minutes.

ingredients.item: 3 Add the fava beans and let them thaw in the pot for 5 minutes. (Or add the lima beans, if using instead, and warm them through for 5 minutes.) Add the dill, salt, pepper, and 2 cups (480 ml) of the broth to the pot. Cover with the lid and cook for 5 minutes.
ingredients.original: 3 Add the fava beans and let them thaw in the pot for 5 minutes. (Or add the lima beans, if using instead, and warm them through for 5 minutes.) Add the dill, salt, pepper, and 2 cups (480 ml) of the broth to the pot. Cover with the lid and cook for 5 minutes.

ingredients.item: 4 Add the remaining 2 cups (480 ml) broth to the pot and bring to a boil over high heat. Taste and adjust the liquid for more salt if needed; it should taste noticeably salty.
ingredients.original: 4 Add the remaining 2 cups (480 ml) broth to the pot and bring to a boil over high heat. Taste and adjust the liquid for more salt if needed; it should taste noticeably salty.

ingredients.item: 5 Add the rice to the pot along with the remaining 2 tablespoons oil and bring to a boil again for 2 to 3 minutes. The liquid level should be about even with the surface of the rice (not much more). Cover the pot, reduce the heat to low, and let cook for 20 minutes, undisturbed.
ingredients.original: 5 Add the rice to the pot along with the remaining 2 tablespoons oil and bring to a boil again for 2 to 3 minutes. The liquid level should be about even with the surface of the rice (not much more). Cover the pot, reduce the heat to low, and let cook for 20 minutes, undisturbed.

ingredients.item: 6 Fluff the rice with a fork or wooden spoon and check on it. If it is still slightly undercooked, add ¼ cup (60 ml) of water, cover it again, and let cook for 10 more minutes before serving.
ingredients.original: 6 Fluff the rice with a fork or wooden spoon and check on it. If it is still slightly undercooked, add ¼ cup (60 ml) of water, cover it again, and let cook for 10 more minutes before serving.
